<b>OPEN SOURCE SIGNAL — Daily Signal #000</b>
<i>13 May 2026</i>

Today’s signals:

1. 📡 Signal One
<b>Telecom surveillance is moving into the infrastructure layer</b>
<i>Citizen Lab documented telecom-linked surveillance activity involving covert location-tracking operations and mobile-network signalling infrastructure.</i>
Source: Citizen Lab

2. 🛰️ Surveillance Watch
<b>Ad-based geolocation is the dark twin of OSINT</b>
<i>Citizen Lab’s Webloc report links ad-based geolocation products to law-enforcement and intelligence customers.</i>
Source: Citizen Lab

3. 🗺️ Map Room
<b>How to verify damage when premium satellite imagery is limited</b>
<i>Bellingcat tracked reported attacks on Iranian police infrastructure using Sentinel-2, PlanetScope where available, OpenStreetMap, Wikimapia, Google Maps and Overpass Turbo.</i>
Source: Bellingcat

4. 🔎 Platform Watch
<b>Xiaohongshu / RedNote deserves a place in platform OSINT</b>
<i>Bellingcat published a guide to Xiaohongshu / RedNote, emphasizing language-specific search, user IDs, platform conventions and the difference between domestic and international visibility.</i>
Source: Bellingcat

5. 🛡️ Investigator OPSEC
<b>Email threats are shifting into identity attacks</b>
<i>Microsoft’s Q1 2026 email threat report focuses on credential phishing, QR-code phishing, CAPTCHA-gated campaigns, adversary-in-the-middle techniques and shifts after disruption of Tycoon2FA.</i>
Source: Microsoft Threat Intelligence

6. 🌐 Infrastructure Signals
<b>Internet disruptions are becoming conflict signals</b>
<i>Cloudflare Radar’s Q1 2026 review highlights internet disruptions, nationwide shutdowns and attacks affecting cloud infrastructure.</i>
Source: Cloudflare Radar

7. ⚠️ Risk Watch
<b>Dark-web OSINT needs hard ethical boundaries</b>
<i>The OSINT Newsletter issue #105 focuses on dark-web OSINT, including tools, strategies, limitations and ways of connecting dark-web signals to surface-web context.</i>
Source: The OSINT Newsletter

<a href="https://deisign.github.io/open-source-signal/issues/open-source-signal-2026-05-13.html">Full issue</a>
